factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities increased from the first six months of 2018 to the first six months of 2019. This was due to higher spending on property, plant and equipment.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Investing Activities

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging through dividend payments and share buybacks.

evidence.text: Net cash used in financing activities in the first six months of 2019 consisted of payments of dividends, which were partially offset by proceeds from the exercise of stock options. During the first six months of 2019, we did not purchase any shares of our common stock.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in certain end markets.

evidence.text: Certain markets, such as oil and gas, softened several months ago, and in the second quarter of 2019 we saw that slowing spread to additional areas, such as heavy machinery and smaller construction customers.

factors.summary: The company has successfully raised prices to mitigate cost increases.

evidence.text: We have implemented a number of pricing actions that are influencing the current period. We instituted a broad round of product price increases at the end of the fourth quarter of 2017 followed by a second, more limited, round at the end of the second quarter of 2018.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products and customers.

evidence.text: Larger customers, for which national accounts are a good proxy and whose more focused buying patterns allow us to offer them better pricing, grew faster than smaller customers.

factors.summary: The oil and gas market has softened, while other markets like heavy machinery and construction are slowing.

evidence.text: Certain markets, such as oil and gas, softened several months ago, and in the second quarter of 2019 we saw that slowing spread to additional areas, such as heavy machinery and smaller construction customers.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly due to tariffs and inflation, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: While we have been successful in raising prices, these increases have lagged behind the rise in product costs over the same period, creating a price/cost deficit that pushes our gross profit margin lower.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by higher wages and bonuses.

evidence.text: Our employee-related expenses increased in the first six months of 2019. This was primarily related to: (1) an increase in our full-time equivalent ('FTE') headcount, (2) moderate increases in hourly base wages, and (3) higher bonuses and commissions due to growth in net sales and net earnings.

factors.summary: Management is cautiously optimistic, investing in growth initiatives despite slowing demand.

evidence.text: We have implemented a number of pricing actions that are influencing the current period. We instituted a broad round of product price increases at the end of the fourth quarter of 2017 followed by a second, more limited, round at the end of the second quarter of 2018.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include slowing demand in certain end markets and rising input costs.

evidence.text: Certain markets, such as oil and gas, softened several months ago, and in the second quarter of 2019 we saw that slowing spread to additional areas, such as heavy machinery and smaller construction customers.

factors.summary: The company is executing a clear long-term strategy focused on growth drivers like Onsite locations and vending devices.

evidence.text: The second quarter of 2019 was a milestone quarter as we opened our 1,000th Onsite and installed our 100,000th vending device (including slightly more than 15,000 devices deployed as part of a lease locker program) in the period.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include tariffs, general inflation, and geopolitical factors affecting certain markets.

evidence.text: These tariffs affect a meaningful number of our products both directly as a result of the tariffs, and indirectly as a result of this action's effect on generalized inflation.

factors.summary: Milestone achievements in Onsite locations and vending devices are near-term catalysts.

evidence.text: The second quarter of 2019 was a milestone quarter as we opened our 1,000th Onsite and installed our 100,000th vending device (including slightly more than 15,000 devices deployed as part of a lease locker program) in the period.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly through growth initiatives like Onsite locations and national accounts.

evidence.text: We signed 110 new national account contracts during the first six months of 2019; 51 of these were signed in the second quarter of 2019. Daily sales from our national account customers grew 14.6% in the first six months of 2019 over the first six months of 2018, and grew 12.5% in the second quarter of 2019 over the second quarter of 2018.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include its strong customer relationships and distribution network.

evidence.text: Our growth drivers have evolved and changed, and can be expected to continue to evolve and change, over time.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is a risk as a few large customers contribute significantly to revenue.

evidence.text: We signed 110 new national account contracts during the first six months of 2019; 51 of these were signed in the second quarter of 2019. Daily sales from our national account customers grew 14.6% in the first six months of 2019 over the first six months of 2018, and grew 12.5% in the second quarter of 2019 over the second quarter of 2018.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are mixed, with some markets experiencing slowdowns while others remain stable.

evidence.text: In April and May of 2019 (June was unavailable at the time of this document's release), this measure was up 1.3% versus the second quarter of 2018, a deceleration from up 2.8% in the first quarter of 2019 versus the first quarter of 2018, and up 4.0% in the fourth quarter of 2018 versus the fourth quarter of 2017.

factors.summary: Higher interest rates are increasing net interest expense.

evidence.text: Our net interest expense was $7.5 in the first six months of 2019 and $3.6 in the second quarter of 2019, compared to $5.7 in the first six months of 2018 and $3.1 in the second quarter of 2018. These increases were mainly caused by higher average interest rates and a higher average debt balance during the period.

factors.summary: Currency fluctuations have a minor impact on daily sales.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales noted above for both 2019 and 2018 was driven primarily by higher unit sales and, to a lesser degree, higher prices to mitigate the effect of general inflation and tariffs in the marketplace. These positive factors were slightly offset by adverse weather across many of our northern regions in the first three months of both six-month periods that created temporary disruptions in activity and reduced sales growth by an estimated 30 to 50 basis points in the first six months of 2019 and 20 to 40 basis points in the first six months of 2018.

factors.summary: Geopolitical tensions, particularly trade relations with China, are impacting the company through tariffs and cost pressures.

evidence.text: We estimate the contribution of price increases to sales growth in the first six months and second quarter of 2019 was 70 to 120 basis points and 20 to 60 basis points in the first six months and second quarter of 2018. We revised the impact of pricing on first quarter 2018 results from our original 50 to 100 basis point estimate based on a review of our methodology conducted during the fourth quarter of 2018.

factors.summary: The company is in growth mode, increasing headcount to support customer growth.

evidence.text: During the last twelve months, we increased our absolute employee headcount by 684 people in our in-market locations and 1,377 people in total. The increase is mostly a function of additions we have made to support customer growth in the field as well as investments in our growth drivers.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, supported by strong operating cash flow.

evidence.text: Cash flow activity was as follows for the periods ended June 30 : Six-month Period 2019 2018 Net cash provided by operating activities $ 333.0 $ 311.6 Percentage of net earnings 83.5 % 80.8 %

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth appears to be mixed, with evidence of slight growth in net earnings and cash flow but a deceleration in net sales compared to prior periods.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased in the first six months of 2019 relative to the first six months of 2018, primarily due to a slight growth in net earnings and a reduced drag from working capital investment relative to what was experienced in the first six months of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: The rate of growth slowed in the second quarter of 2019 versus the first quarter of 2019 and the activity we experienced through much of 2018.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased sales, business activity, market demand, and successful growth initiatives. The evidence indicates sustained growth across different periods and business segments.

evidence.text: The growth in our net accounts receivable from June 30, 2018 to June 30, 2019 reflects sustained sales growth combined with relatively stronger growth of our national accounts business, which tends to have longer payment terms than our business as a whole.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: Daily sales through our vending devices grew at a mid-teens pace in the first six months of 2019 and at a low-teens pace in the second quarter of 2019 when compared to the same periods of 2018 due primarily to the increase in the installed base.

factors.summary: The company's margins are under pressure, with both gross and operating margins compressing. Gross margins have declined due to cost increases and pricing challenges, while operating margins are also facing downward pressure from increased capital expenditures.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased in the first six months of 2019 relative to the first six months of 2018, primarily due to a slight growth in net earnings and a reduced drag from working capital investment relative to what was experienced in the first six months of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: In the first six months of 2019, our gross profit, as a percentage of net sales, declined to 47.3%, or 140 basis points from 48.7% in the first six months of 2018.
